evidence.quote: Today, my guest is Mark Pincus, founder of Zynga, who has arguably created more successful consumer products than anyone else in history, over a dozen both within Zynga and before Zynga.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: With that, I bring you Mark Pincus.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: Before we get into it, don't forget to check out lennysproductpass.com for a free year of the hottest and most well-crafted AI products in the world available exclusively to Lenny's Newsletter subscribers.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: Learn more at vanta.com/lenny.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: That's vanta.com/lenny.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: It could be you're in Marin and I'm like, "Lenny, let's hang out."
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: Zynga
entities.how_described: 本集主要讨论的嘉宾创立的公司，被描述为创造过大量成功消费品（如《FarmVille》和《Words With Friends》），以高留存率和高命中率著称。

evidence.quote: Today, my guest is Mark Pincus, founder of Zynga, who has arguably created more successful consumer products than anyone else in history, over a dozen both within Zynga and before Zynga.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: Well, this is a framework that we got to early on at Zynga and it became like a religion and the fundamental, the core principles and engine behind how we did product management at Zynga.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: But Proven, Better, New is this core philosophy that I've had around products since I started Zynga, which is that we have these instincts in our gut, these human level instincts that are pretty much always right.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: I mean, at Zynga, we'd see new game launches.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And 10 minutes after the game came out, the Zynga PMs emailed around their analysis and they said it's dead on arrival because his FTUE, his first time user experience was so many clicks and so bad that no one was ever going to see his great game design.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Even Sid Meiers tripped over what were understood by the most junior product managers at Zynga was the best of breed approach to onboarding a new user to the first time user experience on the Facebook platform.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And I like to say, and I said this to my product makers at Zynga, if you're truly ambitious, burn your resume.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And he lived in my neighborhood and he was friends with my dog, Zynga, more than he was friends with me.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Or do you want to do what OMGPOP did, which is very similar story but opposite path, which was OMGPOP created the hit game Draw Something and Zynga bought them.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And then I was so humbled and I was in this abyss we can talk about for so long and I was so just desperate to get out of that abyss that by the time I got to Zynga, I did something that was embarrassingly small.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: I err on the side of transparency and honesty and being authentic to a fault and I burn out people in that process and I did at Zynga and I have since because people will say, the negative critique I'm working with me is, "Oh, working for Mark is like third grade soccer and every Monday he comes in excited about a different idea."
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And it was so often at Zynga, it blew my mind.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: I mean, I'll give you one story from Zynga.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: For a lot of product builders, when I think Zynga, they're not like, "This is the product I want to build."
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: There's a whole other story, but I think the part you're getting to is that Zynga was in a lot of ways a victim of its own success, that our games were so viral and took over the feeds on Facebook so much that your friend who was playing it started to bug you as a person not playing it and that's fair.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: But what I would say, I think what's misunderstood about Zynga from the beginning and why we succeeded and we created eight massive hits out of 10 mass large game launches.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And I think the two things to take away from Zynga's success in my opinion that I hold is to have a strong innovation vision that translates to what your teams are doing every week.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And in 2007 when I started Zynga, video gaming was a $23 billion business, but I had stopped playing games.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Today and by the way, with Zynga, I said, "I'm going to go to the cocktail party.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: It's what led me to Zynga.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Our demand will too, but if today we start building, re-imagining consumer services based on free tokens, I think that's such an interesting innovation zone and it could sound like a dot-com business plan when I remember ordering pig ears from my dog, Zynga, and wanting to send thank you notes to the VCs because they were cheaper than at Petco.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: I believe that, and I did that building Zynga to a fault.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: But when I was building Zynga, we had to apologize for things like micromanaging, or expecting massive results from our teams.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: I wanted to write a book on parenting saying, "Everything I know about parenting, I learned from my dog Zynga."
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: It took me until I started Zynga at 41 to finally identify and articulate my why.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: Proven, Better, New
entities.how_described: 本集核心拆解的产品构建框架：Proven（合法复制已验证的最佳方案）、Better（让现有用户觉得“太棒了，我要换”）、New（吸引人尝试但极可能失败的全新卖点）。

evidence.quote: I want to start with this framework that you developed called Proven, Better, New.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: And it was so fundamental to the book that for three of the four years writing the book, we called it Proven, Better, New.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: But Proven, Better, New is this core philosophy that I've had around products since I started Zynga, which is that we have these instincts in our gut, these human level instincts that are pretty much always right.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: The framework of Proven, Better, New takes that philosophy and says, "Okay, what do we do with that?"
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: So the point of Proven, Better, New is to say, "Let's take all the proven off the table."
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And if we do Proven, Better, New right, the product has much better odds of succeeding in the market, not failing for the wrong reasons.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: I like to think if you do Proven, Better, New right, it's like a time machine because what if I could go back to Mark 2003 and '4 and say, "Dude, you don't even realize how right you are.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: If you're doing Proven, Better New, you're also looking around you for what are you finding heat that's proven in the market that you can also go test?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: So kind of the steps that I hear is, so Proven, Better, New.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

evidence.quote: And that what I've found over the years with founders is that they start to use Proven, Better, New to justify a wrong idea.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And yet the masters at this product craft do Proven, Better, New, whether they call it that or not, and they do it at such a beautiful level that nobody even thinks ... No one realizes what it's derivative of.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Is every product from Proven, Better, New?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And now they were ruthlessly, desperately in need of a hit and now they did Proven, Better, New perfectly.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 留存率 (retention)
entities.how_described: 本集被反复强调为消费品成功的关键指标，嘉宾认为最有价值的公司都拥有最高的第365天留存率（Day 365 retention），以此取代短期的病毒式传播。

evidence.quote: So there was a point in Tribe where I knew my metrics weren't working, our D30 retention was terrible.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: I think if I'm unpacking that when you say that the products turn people off, it's funny you say that because FarmVille and CityVille were our biggest hits in terms of installs, engagement, retention, revenues.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And then if you think at a mechanical metric level, our core metric was retention, not virality.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: We grew, we outlasted everybody because we had the best retention.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: I think we were the only consumer company in the world that tracked Day 365 retention.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: I believe that the most valuable companies in the world, well, statistically this is true, they have the highest Day 365 retention.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Can you build against Day 365 retention?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: We can find early indicators of a positive Day 365 and a negative Day 365 retention.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: But we were tracking both in our games, in our network, what was the retention?
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 智能体 (agent)
entities.how_described: 本集将其视为未来服务与社交的核心：既可以是24小时待命的旅行代理，也可以作为信任的“社交膜”，在孤独的 AI 时代重新连接人与人。

evidence.quote: I love the idea of the agent being in the middle on brokering our social relationships and getting back to that lead gen, dating, jobs, general listings.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: But I love the idea that the agent has our context and knows my context and your context and we have this membrane, I call it the social membrane, this intelligent membrane of trust and it gets smarter and smarter at knowing when to change dynamically change that trust or change that attention level that it's going to take from us like a great chief of staff and it could share less information with both of us than it knows.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: There's some really interesting spaces that the AI and the agent can broker between us in ways that start to give us both productivity.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: One example that is my number one is my Easter egg on this is I think there should be an agentic travel agent.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: So a perfect example to me of a place where I think there is latent demand and the only reason we don't have it is because it doesn't make economic sense to offer it to us because we won't pay enough for it is a travel agent.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: I believe if I offered you a free 24/7 travel agent that was always there for you, knows your travel context, knows you, and will actually not just book travel, but I think the most valuable part is when you're in the middle of a travel, a trip, be ready to rebook your flight and be on top of your travel logistics as they're happening and often failing.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: But if someone built the best travel agent today, maybe we'd find out about it, but it's a tough road.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: AI 聊天 (AI chat)
entities.how_described: 本集指出目前的 AI 聊天（如 GPT 或 Claude）虽然是通往新技术的重要入口，但还没有成为一个真正的平台，导致应用分发极为困难。

evidence.quote: We have a new kind of portal to that in GPT or whatever chat we use, AI chat, but it's not in my mind a platform.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: There's a lot of generic value I get through AI chat today that I'm having more and more trouble differentiating and it's just I don't even know anymore if I'm asking a question to Claude or to GPT.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 产品市场契合度 (product market fit)
entities.how_described: 本集认为，想要找到它，创始人必须放下自尊和过度的雄心，从一个“小得让人尴尬”的切入点开始起步和试错。

evidence.quote: And I would say there's so many paradoxes in this and one that I've had to learn over and over again is if we're too ambitious at the outset and too ambitious and visionary about the product we want to build, then we will probably miss product market fit because we won't start at a small enough, humble enough place because it might ... Usually the starting point for these products is embarrassingly small.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And the irony for me is I've found that my career has gone in these waves and maybe some of your other founders have had this experience too, that I've started in a very humble place of, "I just want to get to anything, any product market fit", had bigger success than I imagined I would have in my first two companies, Freeloader and Support.com.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: It's too easy to raise money and recruit teams against a big vision before we've gotten to product market fit, but the world doesn't care about your resume.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: Even if we could get funding, even if it has some traction, we have a north star that we are not going to stop until we find product market fit against our north star and this isn't it and it's going to take courage on our part.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: I got so into wanting to prove I could build this experience that I was building this game engine, web browser-based game engine and I was not getting a product market fit.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 微管理 (micromanagement)
entities.how_described: 本集反直觉地提出“微观管理是美好的”，主张创始人应贴近底层（close to the metal），尽可能久地亲自抓像素级的产品细节，而不是把时间浪费在见投资人上。

evidence.quote: You have this line in the book along these lines, "Micromanagement is beautiful.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_1

entities.name: 虚拟机 (virtual machine)
entities.how_described: 本集在讨论 Bolt.new 爆火案例时提到，创始人将他们之前在 Web 端做的虚拟机加到了 AI 编程副驾中，从而做出了比其他人都好的产品。

evidence.quote: And then they had this realization one day of, "Wait a second, if we take these web stacks, this virtual machine that we've been making work on the web, but we actually add that to an AI coding copilot, we now have something that's better than what anybody else has."
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 元宇宙 (metaverse)
entities.how_described: 本集提到嘉宾将其定义为“模糊虚拟与现实的界限”，并提到他四次终止了自己耗资数千万美元的元宇宙项目 do.Earth。

evidence.quote: I've been building my version of the metaverse for 20 years.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: I call the metaverse that I want life at the speed of play.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: And by the way, I define the metaverse and I give Reid Hoffman credit for this definition as blurring the lines between the virtual and the reel.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: So we're getting more and more metaverse in.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

entities.name: 批判性思维 (critical thinking)
entities.how_described: 本集在育儿话题中被提及，嘉宾认为在 AI 时代，流水线式的知识教育已经结束，比逼孩子考大学更重要的是教给他们批判性思维。

evidence.quote: And what I've tried to do with my older kids is teach them to have critical thinking more than... And I say, "I don't care if you go to college," which is a little hard for them because they're achievers.
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0

evidence.quote: What I care about is that you develop critical thinking, and you find a way to be useful to people in the world."
evidence.speaker: SPEAKER_0
